sections.recommendation:
Conduct a public hearing and upon conclusion, adopt a Resolution approving new fees
and increasing current fees for select Marina fees; and rescinding Resolution No.
71,840-N.S. and all amendatory resolutions.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The cumulative impact of these fee increases is estimated to add $247,500 in annual
revenue to the Marina Fund (Fund 608), which supports Waterfront operations including
the Marina, facilities, roads, recreation programs and special events, (see Table 1).
These increases are needed to offset the costs of providing these services and to help
ensure the long-term financial sustainability of the Waterfront. As discussed at the
Council Budget & Finance Policy Committee meeting on October 28, 2025, the Marina
Fund has had operating deficits for several years, and the Council has had to address
these shortfalls with a variety of General Fund balancing measures.1 The Marina Fund
was buoyed by the passage of the 2024 Parks Tax increase, which shifted more than
$1.5M in costs for Waterfront parks and landscaped areas off the Marina Fund.
However, the weak recovery of the regional hotel market since COVID has created
significant uncertainty for the Marina Fund’s single largest revenue source, the
Doubletree Hotel.
These fee increases are needed as one part of the City’s strategy to balance the Marina
Fund in the next budget cycle. Other measures include Waterfront recreation fee
increases2, revenue from updated Waterfront leases, and increased berth occupancy at
the Marina. If these fees are not adopted, the Marina Fund is likely to generate deficits
which will either require additional reductions to address any shortfall or additional
General Fund subsidy which may not be feasible given the projected fiscal constraints
of the General Fund.
1 See https://berkeleyca.gov/sites/default/files/legislative-body-meeting-attachments/2025-10-
28%20Item%2003%20Marina%20Fund%20Update%20Presentation.pdf
2 See Council report on Recreation Fee Increases, 1/20/26.

sections.current_situation:
On June 24, 2025, Council adopted an updated Marina fee (Resolution No. 71,840-
N.S.). The City regularly reviews all Marina fees in an effort to keep facilities and
infrastructure accessible to the public while accounting for increased costs to deliver
those services. Proposed fee changes described below are proposed to go into effect
January 28, 2026, unless otherwise noted.
 Berth Fees are the monthly slip fee for slipholders with a permit to berth. Fees
are proposed to increase by 3% for each of the next two years: a 3% increase in
FY27, and a 3% increase in FY28. This is to better recover operational costs,
while staying competitive with the local market for slips, (see Tables 2 and 3).

sections.background:
The Marina Fund covers all Waterfront operations including the Marina, landscaping,
facilities, parks, roads, recreation programs and special events. Specific examples
include the daily maintenance of over 1,000 berths, repairs to docks, pilings and
buildings, staffing in Shorebird Nature Center and Adventure playground and the daily
administration of what essentially is a “small city”. These costs are paid via revenues
from berth fees, special fees, and commercial leases and licenses. Berth fees comprise
46% of all Marina Fund revenue, while all other marina fees combined comprise
approximately 9% of Marina Fund revenue.
The proposed fees were brought before the Parks, Recreation & Waterfront
Commission on November 12, 2025 at their regular meeting. These fees along with a
range of financial alternatives to fiscally stabilize the Marina Fund were presented to the
Council Budget & Finance Policy Committee on October 28, 2025.3
sections.environmental:
The proposed fee increases will improve the City’s ability to manage and operate the
Berkeley Waterfront, where the City provides unique recreational and educational
opportunities for the community to engage with and learn about the Bay and local
marine environment.
3 See https://berkeleyca.gov/sites/default/files/legislative-body-meeting-attachments/2025-10-
28%20Item%2003%20Marina%20Fund%20Update%20Presentation.pdf

Page 7 of 15
Page 8 of 15
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ESTABLISHING FEES FOR GOODS AND SERVICES PROVIDED AT THE
BERKELEY MARINA AND RESCINDING RESOLUTION NO. 71,840-N.S.
WHEREAS, on June 24, 2025, Council adopted the new Marina Fee Schedule
(Resolution No. 71,840-N.S.); and
WHEREAS, the City’s costs to operate facilities, provide services and undertake capital
improvements at the Berkeley Marina have increased with inflation, rising staff costs,
and aging infrastructure; and
WHEREAS, Marina fees are required to offset those costs and the additional revenue
will be deposited in the Marina Fund (608); and
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
attached rate structure (Exhibit A) is hereby established for use by the general public for
facilities and services at the Berkeley Marina, effective January 28, 2026.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that Resolution No. 71,840-N.S. and all amendatory
resolutions are hereby rescinded effective January 27, 2026.
